stance: Risk-on but beta-aware: overweight the India capex / power-T&D / electrification cycle and select rising-revision metals, funded by exiting refuted cyclicals and the illiquid Vedanta demerger stubs.
experience_note: Pre-registered watch-list (anti-hindsight): (1) The capex/power-T&D thesis is right only if revisions and order-book momentum PERSIST — if T&D/capital-goods revisions roll over (the way ABB's and L&T's already have here) while I'm overweight, I am wrong and must cut, not average down. (2) My metals sleeve is a cyclical bet on revisions, not a value bet — if metals revisions fade I exit even if the names look 'cheap'. (3) Sterlite Tech is an 8-sigma momentum name; I sized it tactical on purpose — a sharp momentum break is my signal I overstayed. (4) I am watching my realised beta: track record beta is already ~1.17, so if this book pushes beta materially higher without commensurate excess return, the tilt is poorly transferred (long-only transfer leak) and I rebalance toward the quality ballast. What would change my mind broadly: a regime turn where defensives/quality lead and capex cyclicals de-rate — then the structural sleeve becomes a drag and I rotate to the Gland/Zydus/LIC/Nestle/ICICI ballast.
guardrail_notes: dropped 10 non-priceable/unknown ticker(s): ASTRAL, SCHAEFFLER, AJANTPHARM, PHOENIXLTD, BLUESTARCO, EMCURE, KPRMILL, SBFC
